1. A 32-year-old woman at 36 weeks gestation presents with a blood pressure of 158/102 mmHg on two separate occasions, 4 hours apart. Urinalysis shows 2+ proteinuria. She denies headache or visual changes. Fetal heart rate tracing is reactive. What is the most appropriate next step?
Expectant management with weekly monitoring
Administer antihypertensives and plan delivery at 37 weeks
Immediate cesarean delivery
Initiate magnesium sulfate and discharge home
Administer antihypertensives and plan delivery at 37 weeks
Preeclampsia with features of severe hypertension (≥160/110) requires antihypertensive therapy and delivery is recommended at 37 weeks gestation. Magnesium sulfate seizure prophylaxis should also be initiated. Expectant management beyond 37 weeks increases maternal and fetal risk without benefit.
